morning.title: Morning Brief — May 21, 2026
morning.content:
**"Courage is the ladder on which all the other virtues mount."**
— Clare Boothe Luce

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Capitol police officers who defended the building on January 6th filed suit this week to block payouts from the DOJ's new $1.776 billion "Anti-Weaponization Fund" — a settlement pool created after Trump's IRS lawsuit, intended to compensate those claiming politically motivated prosecution, including some rioters.

**HVAC & Industry:** HVAC-focused Madison Air Solutions pulled off a $2.2 billion IPO this spring, hitting a $13+ billion valuation — a strong signal that investors now view HVAC, especially indoor air quality and precision airflow, as critical infrastructure rather than a commodity business.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 20, 2026
morning.content:
**"Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do."**
— Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** In the biggest primary night of 2026, Trump scored a decisive win Tuesday as Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky — one of his most vocal Republican critics in Congress — was ousted by a Trump-backed challenger, cementing the president's grip on the GOP ahead of the midterms.

**HVAC & Industry:** Consolidation keeps rolling — Redwood Services acquired Las Vegas-based Sierra Platform, while Rocket Group picked up two more regional players: True Blue Mechanical in Delaware and TLS Air Conditioning & Insulation in Tampa.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 19, 2026
morning.content:
**"The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek."**
— Joseph Campbell

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** President Trump announced a deal to end the Iran war, with ceasefire negotiations active and live updates ongoing — the biggest foreign policy development in months. Separately, three people were killed in a shooting at a San Diego Islamic center that authorities are investigating as a hate crime.

**HVAC & Industry:** Carrier Global posted Q1 2026 sales of $5.34 billion, up 2.3% year-over-year, signaling steady demand. Industry consolidation is accelerating — Redwood Services and Rocket Group both made acquisitions in the past week, snapping up regional HVAC and mechanical contractors.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 18, 2026
morning.content:
*"Anyone can hold the helm when the sea is calm."*
— Publilius Syrus

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Two Navy fighter jets collided mid-air during an air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho — both crews ejected safely. Witnesses called it incredibly lucky; the incident is under investigation.

**HVAC & Industry:** Madison Air Solutions completed a $2.2 billion IPO this month, hitting a $13 billion valuation. Investors are treating HVAC — especially IAQ and ventilation — as critical infrastructure, with demand surging from data centers and healthcare.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 17, 2026
morning.content:
**"It is not because things are difficult that we do not dare; it is because we do not dare that things are difficult."**
— Seneca

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** President Trump wrapped up a historic three-day visit to China this weekend, with talks unexpectedly dominated by Taiwan discussions and the announcement of a new framework for managing the U.S.-China relationship — the most significant diplomatic development between the two powers in years.

**HVAC & Industry:** Madison Air Solutions raised $2.2 billion in an IPO this spring, hitting a $13B+ valuation — a clear signal that investors see HVAC as critical infrastructure, with demand surging from data centers, healthcare, and advanced manufacturing driving premium system sales.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 16, 2026
morning.content:
**"The longest journey is the journey inward."**
— Dag Hammarskjöld

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** President Trump wrapped up a three-day trip to China dominated by unexpected discussions over Taiwan, with Trump notably declining to push back on Xi Jinping's rhetoric — a significant development that rattled allies and produced a new framework for managing the US-China relationship.

**HVAC & Industry:** Madison Air Solutions raised over $2.2 billion in a blockbuster IPO this spring, hitting a $13B+ valuation — a signal that investors now view HVAC, particularly indoor air quality and precision airflow, as critical infrastructure on par with data centers and healthcare.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 15, 2026
morning.content:
**"To dare is to lose one's footing momentarily. To not dare is to lose oneself."**
— Søren Kierkegaard

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** President Trump wrapped his trip to Beijing claiming major trade deals with China, with both sides describing the visit as a significant breakthrough on economic agreements. Separately, the FBI is offering a $200,000 reward for a former Air Force counterintelligence officer charged with spying for Iran — she defected in 2013 and remains at large.

**HVAC & Industry:** Madison Air Solutions completed a $2.2 billion IPO this spring, hitting a $13 billion valuation — a strong signal that investors view HVAC and indoor air quality as critical infrastructure. Could attract more capital and M&A activity across the sector.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 14, 2026
morning.content:
*"Behold the turtle. He makes progress only when he sticks his neck out."*
— James Bryant Conant

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** China's Xi Jinping directly warned Trump that disputes over Taiwan could escalate into conflict as diplomatic and trade tensions remain elevated. Separately, a cargo ship was reportedly seized off the UAE coast and is heading toward Iran, raising fresh concerns in the Strait of Hormuz.

**HVAC & Industry:** Madison Air Solutions completed a $2.2 billion IPO this spring at a $13 billion valuation — a signal that investors now see HVAC, particularly indoor air quality and precision airflow, as critical infrastructure well beyond residential comfort.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 13, 2026
morning.content:
*"It is not because things are difficult that we do not dare; it is because we do not dare that they are difficult."*
— Seneca

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Trump arrived in China for high-stakes trade talks as Pete Hegseth testified before the Senate requesting a wartime FY2027 defense budget — and the FDA Commissioner resigned, adding to a turbulent week in Washington.

**HVAC & Industry:** HVAC company Madison Air Solutions completed a $2.2B IPO at a $13B valuation, signaling strong investor confidence in the sector — particularly in IAQ, filtration, and precision airflow systems serving data centers and healthcare.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 12, 2026
morning.content:
*"There are two ways of meeting difficulties: you alter the difficulties, or you alter yourself to meet them."*
— Phyllis Bottome

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Trump is warning that the fragile US-Iran ceasefire is on "life support," with the administration considering restarting Navy military escorts through the Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate. Markets are also on edge ahead of today's fresh inflation data.

**HVAC & Industry:** Madison Air Solutions completed a $2.2 billion IPO this spring, hitting a $13 billion valuation — a strong signal that investors view HVAC (especially IAQ, ventilation, and precision airflow for data centers and healthcare) as critical infrastructure, not just a comfort play.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 11, 2026
morning.content:
**"No bird soars too high if he soars with his own wings."**
— William Blake

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** A hantavirus outbreak tied to a cruise ship is drawing national attention — at least one American repatriated from the ship has tested positive for the Andes strain, and critics are already questioning whether the CDC's response signals a broader readiness problem, though officials maintain the public health risk is low.

**HVAC & Industry:** Another round of manufacturer price increases took effect May 1st, with most brands up 3–5%; Nu-Calgon's glycol products jumped 15% and Reflectix insulation hit as high as 12%, adding more cost pressure heading into the heart of cooling season.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 10, 2026
morning.content:
**"Do not be too timid and squeamish about your actions. All life is an experiment. The more experiments you make the better."**
— Ralph Waldo Emerson

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** The US-Iran standoff is at a critical inflection point — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is awaiting Iran's response to an interim ceasefire proposal, now Day 71 of the Middle East conflict, while sporadic clashes near the Strait of Hormuz continue to test the fragile truce. Trump is also preparing for a rare visit to China this week amid the turbulent backdrop.

**HVAC & Industry:** A fresh wave of manufacturer price increases took effect May 1st, with most brands raising prices 3–5%; standouts include Reflectix (up to 12%), Nu-Calgon glycol products (15%), and DirectedAir select categories (5–14%), driven by tariffs, the A2L refrigerant transition, and supply chain pressure.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 9, 2026
morning.content:
**"Do not wait for ideal circumstances, nor for the best opportunities; they will never come."**
— Janet Erskine Stuart

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Trump announced that Russia and Ukraine have agreed to a 3-day ceasefire and prisoner swap — a significant diplomatic move as the conflict grinds into its fifth year. Separately, Virginia's Supreme Court struck down Democrat-redrawn Congressional maps, handing Republicans a key redistricting win ahead of the 2026 midterms.

**HVAC & Industry:** Another wave of manufacturer price increases landed in May — Carlisle, Sporlan, Systemair, AO Smith, and others are raising prices 3-15% this month, driven by continued tariff pressure and the ongoing A2L refrigerant transition. Worth keeping an eye on margins.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 8, 2026
morning.content:
*"The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing."*
— Marcus Aurelius, Meditations

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** The US-Iran conflict entered Day 69 today with US military strikes on Iranian sites and Iran firing missiles in response, even as Trump insists a ceasefire is still in effect and warned Tehran to sign a peace deal "fast." Iran claims the US violated the ceasefire by targeting vessels and ports; negotiations remain active.

**HVAC & Industry:** HVACR manufacturers rolled out another round of price increases in May, with most in the low-to-mid single-digit range — Carlisle HVAC Brands is raising prices 3–5% across all items effective May 1, with insulation, fittings, valves, and residential ventilation seeing the steepest hikes.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 7, 2026
morning.content:
*"It is not because things are difficult that we do not dare; it is because we do not dare that things are difficult."*
— Seneca

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** President Trump announced a pause on "Project Freedom" — the U.S. operation to move ships through the Strait of Hormuz — at Pakistan's request, while keeping the Navy blockade of Iran in place as nuclear deal negotiations continue. Separately, the U.S. national debt has officially exceeded GDP for the first time since World War II, with public debt hitting $31.27T against a $31.22T GDP.

**HVAC & Industry:** A fresh wave of manufacturer price increases hit May 1st — Carlisle HVAC Brands up 3-5%, Reflectix up 12%, Nu-Calgon glycol products up 15%, and a dozen others ranging 2-14%. Tariff pressure, A2L refrigerant transition costs, and supply chain strain are all cited as drivers.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 6, 2026
morning.content:
**"Anyone can hold the helm when the sea is calm."**
— Publilius Syrus, Roman writer, 1st century BC

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Oil prices fell sharply this morning after President Trump announced "great progress" in negotiations with Iran, with the U.S. pausing its operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz — though the White House faces mounting scrutiny as it insists the conflict is winding down while hostilities continue.

**HVAC & Industry:** Rocket Group (CA) announced a new partnership with San Antonio-based Roadrunner Plumbing & Air, and ServiceTrade (NC) struck a strategic deal with Pine Services Group (SF) — consolidation in the contractor services space keeps accelerating heading into peak season.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 5, 2026
morning.content:
**"The purpose of life is to be defeated by greater and greater things."**
— Rainer Maria Rilke

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** The U.S.-Iran standoff in the Strait of Hormuz intensified overnight — Secretary of War Hegseth held a Pentagon briefing insisting the ceasefire with Iran remains intact despite a spike in Iranian hostilities, while Joint Chiefs Chairman Dan Caine revealed roughly 22,500 mariners are currently trapped in the Persian Gulf. Separately, primary elections are underway today in Ohio and Indiana, with Vivek Ramaswamy making a run at the Republican gubernatorial nomination in Ohio.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 4, 2026
morning.content:
**"Never look down to test the ground before taking your next step; only he who keeps his eye fixed on the far horizon will find the right road."**
— Dag Hammarskjöld

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Inflation climbed to 3.5% year-over-year — the highest in nearly three years — driven by surging energy costs as gas averages $4.39/gallon amid ongoing Iran conflict tensions. Despite the squeeze, GDP grew 2% in Q1, unemployment claims hit multi-decade lows, and markets continue pushing record highs.

**HVAC & Industry:** Major HVAC manufacturers have pushed through broad price increases heading into peak season, driven by tariff pressure, the completed A2L refrigerant transition, and supply chain disruptions — expect continued cost headwinds through summer.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 3, 2026
morning.content:
**"The whole future lies in uncertainty: live immediately."**
— Seneca

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Trump announced Saturday he's pulling troops from Germany well beyond the initially planned 5,000 — saying "we're cutting a lot further" — a sharp escalation of his push to scale back America's NATO commitment that has Germany and European allies on edge.

**Business:** Berkshire Hathaway held its first annual meeting under new CEO Greg Abel yesterday in Omaha — notably smaller crowds than the Buffett years, though Warren still showed up as chairman. The era of folksy wisdom is giving way to hard business talk.

Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 2, 2026
morning.content:
**"First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do."**
— Epictetus

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Nationwide May Day protests swept the country yesterday under a "Workers Over Billionaires" banner, with nearly 500 organizations staging over 750 events — marches, job walkouts, and shopping boycotts — opposing Trump administration policies and rising living costs tied to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

**HVAC & Industry:** HVAC equipment prices are continuing to climb in 2026, driven by a perfect storm of tariff pressure (10% baseline on all imports, up to 145% on Chinese goods, 25% on Mexican products), the completed A2L refrigerant transition, and persistent supply chain disruption — squeezing margins across the board.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — May 1, 2026
morning.content:
*"Anyone can hold the helm when the sea is calm."*

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Hundreds of thousands of Americans are walking out of work and school today for nationwide "Workers Over Billionaires" May Day protests, with demonstrations sweeping major cities and echoing broader labor rallies happening globally.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 30, 2026
morning.content:
*"Anyone can hold the helm when the sea is calm."*
— Publilius Syrus

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 *US News:* The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 yesterday to strike down Louisiana's second majority-Black congressional district, calling it an unconstitutional racial gerrymander — a landmark decision that significantly narrows the Voting Rights Act and could allow Republican-led states to redraw minority-majority districts across the country.

🔧 *HVAC & Industry:* Three new federal antitrust lawsuits filed this week allege major HVACR manufacturers conspired to inflate equipment prices starting in 2020 and continuing to the present — worth watching as it could put pricing pressure and legal scrutiny on the whole industry.

— Wally

morning.content:
**"It is not because things are difficult that we do not dare; it is because we do not dare that things are difficult."**

**US News:** King Charles III is wrapping up a historic state visit to the US — after a White House state dinner Tuesday and a rare joint address to Congress emphasizing the US-UK alliance, he and Queen Camilla head to New York City today, all against a backdrop of tension between the two nations over the ongoing war with Iran.

**HVAC & Industry:** EGIA's 2026 HVAC Contractor Survey dropped this week and the picture is clear: labor shortages, rising costs, and regulatory pressure remain the top headwinds, with nearly every contractor surveyed reporting price increases in the past year — and more companies expanding into plumbing and electrical to drive growth.

— Wally

morning.content:

The visit caps a week of high-stakes diplomacy as the US continues military engagement against Iran, with Gulf states convening emergency talks today in response to recent Iranian strikes.

— Wally

morning.content:
**"The greatest test of courage on earth is to bear defeat without losing heart."**
— Robert Green Ingersoll

**US News:** Day 58 of the U.S.-Iran conflict — Iran's Foreign Minister flew to Moscow this weekend as diplomacy stalls, while the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz holds oil prices above $100/barrel, raising fresh inflation concerns to kick off the week.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 26, 2026
morning.content:
**"A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are for."**
— John A. Shedd

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** A gunman opened fire outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington last night, prompting President Trump and Melania to be evacuated. The suspect, identified as 31-year-old Cole Allen of Torrance, CA, was arrested at the scene; a Secret Service agent was struck but protected by a bulletproof vest and is expected to be OK.

**HVAC & Industry:** EGIA's newly released 2026 HVAC Contractor Survey finds nearly all contractors raised prices in the past year as labor shortages, rising costs, and government regulation continue to squeeze margins — with a growing number expanding into plumbing and electrical to diversify revenue.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 25, 2026
morning.content:
**"He conquers who endures."**
— Persius, Roman poet (34–62 AD)

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Trump is dispatching envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan today for direct talks with Iran's foreign minister, as the ongoing US-Iran conflict continues to disrupt energy exports through the Strait of Hormuz and rattle global markets. Meanwhile, a federal appeals court blocked Trump's executive order suspending asylum access at the southern border, ruling it violates existing immigration law.

**HVAC & Industry:** A new 2026 federal tax law has eliminated key HVAC energy efficiency credits, catching many contractors off guard — industry advisors are urging businesses to restructure finances now to avoid cash flow gaps as the changes take effect.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 24, 2026
morning.content:
**"No man is free who is not master of himself."**
— Epictetus

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** The US-Iran conflict ("Operation Epic Fury") remains the dominant story — Trump delivered a prime-time address signaling the campaign could wrap in weeks, but tensions escalated after a US fighter jet was shot down over Iran, marking the first manned American aircraft downed in the conflict. Meanwhile, the Supreme Court heard oral arguments on Trump's birthright citizenship executive order, with justices appearing skeptical and a decision expected in June.

**HVAC & Industry:** EGIA's 2026 HVAC Contractor Survey found nearly all residential contractors raised prices over the past year, with labor shortages and regulatory pressure still the top threats — and a growing number are expanding into plumbing and electrical as multi-trade growth strategies gain traction.

— Wally

morning.content:

Separately, Canadian PM Mark Carney fired back at Washington, declaring the U.S. does not get to dictate the terms of any trade deal between the two countries.

**HVAC & Industry:** Jetson (Denver/Vancouver) closed a $50 million fundraising round this week to accelerate heat pump adoption across North America — a sign capital is still chasing electrification despite the ongoing A2L refrigerant transition.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 22, 2026
morning.content:
*"If you add a little to a little, and then do it again, soon that little shall be much."*

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire today, giving Tehran more time to come to the negotiating table — but Iran responded by seizing two ships in the Strait of Hormuz, keeping tensions high and oil markets on edge.

**HVAC & Industry:** Jetson announced a $50M fundraising round this week to accelerate heat pump adoption across North America, while Hudson Technologies signed a refrigerant licensing deal for R-448A/R-449A reclamation — both signals that the refrigerant transition and electrification push are picking up real momentum heading into cooling season.

— Wally

**HVAC & Industry:** ACHR News dropped their 2026 Residential Cooling Showcase this month, giving contractors a side-by-side look at new manufacturer offerings heading into peak cooling season — worth a scan if the team is evaluating any equipment lines.

Businesses can now apply for refunds, though legal battles and processing delays may slow actual payouts.

— Wally

morning.content:

**US News:** Iran has fully closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to a US naval blockade, firing on ships as a ceasefire deadline looms — a major escalation that's rattling global energy markets and forcing a critical decision point for US foreign policy.

**HVAC & Industry:** Tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper are squeezing HVAC construction budgets across the board; analysts flagged residential markets as the weakest segment right now, while data centers continue to be the bright spot driving demand.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 18, 2026
morning.content:
**"The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones."**

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

**US News:** Peace deal optimism is building in the Middle East — Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz open and Trump voiced confidence a deal with Tehran is close, though Iran remains cautious. It's the biggest developing story heading into the weekend.

**HVAC & Industry:** Tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper are squeezing HVAC project budgets industry-wide, with data centers, hospitals, and labs hit hardest due to heavy copper use. Early procurement and regional sourcing are the plays right now.

morning.content:

Separately, newly unsealed California court records allege Amazon systematically pressured third-party sellers to raise prices on rival platforms like Walmart and Target to make Amazon appear cheaper.

morning.content:
**"The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones."**

**US News:** The US-Israel-Iran conflict remains the dominant story, with a second round of peace talks expected today, April 16. Trump stated Iran is "eager to negotiate" after receiving backlash from China over a potential Strait of Hormuz blockade.

**HVAC & Industry:** BNP Paribas flagged mixed performance in the US HVAC sector this week — residential demand is soft, but long-term tailwinds from electrification and AI data center cooling are strong. Trane Technologies and Vertiv are both positioned as beneficiaries.

morning.content:

**HVAC & Industry:** ACHR News just dropped its April 2026 price increase list — aluminum products are up as much as 15% and copper fittings up 12%, with Robertshaw averaging a 6% hike. Notably, it's also the first time the list includes a price *decrease* from any manufacturer, which may signal some tariff pressure is starting to ease.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 8, 2026
morning.content:
**"The first step toward success is taken when you refuse to be a captive of the environment in which you first find yourself."**
— Mark Caine

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — The U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire Tuesday, hours before Trump's strike deadline, with Trump suspending attacks contingent on Iran's "immediate" reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and progress on a 10-point nuclear proposal from Tehran. Reports of continued Israeli strikes are already complicating the fragile pause.

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — Penn State researchers published a study showing AI-driven software can cut data center cooling energy use by up to 25% by dynamically shifting power loads to periods of favorable weather and cheaper electricity — a direct play on the exploding demand for smarter building systems in mission-critical facilities.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 7, 2026
morning.content:
**"Courage is not simply one of the virtues, but the form of every virtue at the testing point."**
— C.S. Lewis

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — The U.S.-Israel war against Iran escalated overnight, with American forces striking military targets on Kharg Island and reports of a Tehran synagogue being destroyed in the strikes. Trump issued a stark warning on Truth Social as his deadline for a nuclear deal with Iran looms, with "major combat operations" now in their sixth week.

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — The 2026 AHR Expo spotlighted heat pumps and dual-fuel chillers as the commercial HVAC industry accelerates toward decarbonization, with data center cooling demand driving manufacturers to prioritize compact, high-efficiency designs using low-GWP refrigerants.

⚠️ Bonus — Georgia is holding a congressional runoff today between Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller; the outcome will be watched as an early 2026 midterm temperature check.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 6, 2026
morning.content:
**"The secret of getting ahead is getting started."**
— Mark Twain

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — Iran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Trump's Monday 10am ET deadline, and the US-Iran conflict (now five weeks in, dubbed "Operation Epic Fury") is escalating — gas prices are spiking domestically as a result. Separately, Trump fired AG Pam Bondi and replaced her with Deputy AG Todd Blanch, and a new CNN poll shows only 31% of Americans approve of Trump's handling of the economy, down from 39% in January.

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — Bloom Energy's 2026 Data Center Power Report confirms AI-driven compute demand is outpacing grid capacity, with widening interconnection timelines and growing demand for on-site power alternatives — a real tailwind for building systems and energy infrastructure businesses.

⚠️ Bonus — Trump is pushing a record $1.5 trillion Pentagon budget, including a "Golden Dome" missile defense system and major increases for submarines and fighter aircraft — the largest defense spend proposal in U.S. history.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 5, 2026
morning.content:
**"It is not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves."**
— Sir Edmund Hillary

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — Trump confirmed both crew members of the F-15E shot down over Iran have been rescued following a daring special forces and CIA operation — he called it "one of the most daring Search and Rescue Operations in US History." Iran says 5 were killed in the strikes; Trump is now threatening to hit Iranian power plants by Tuesday unless Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz by 10am ET Monday.

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — The Strait of Hormuz standoff is the one to watch for energy. If Iran doesn't reopen by Monday's deadline, oil and energy price spikes could hit commercial building operating costs and data center power markets hard heading into Q2.

⚠️ Bonus — This is the most significant U.S. military escalation in years. Monday's 10am ET deadline is a real line in the sand — Tuesday could get loud.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 4, 2026
morning.content:
**"Do not pray for easy lives. Pray to be stronger men."**
— John F. Kennedy

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — Iran shot down two U.S. military aircraft Friday in separate attacks — the first time American planes have been downed since the conflict began five weeks ago. One crew member has been rescued; a second remains missing with a search-and-rescue operation underway. Trump said the incidents won't affect Iran negotiations, calling it simply: "It's war."

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — The Iran conflict is squeezing global energy markets, with oil prices elevated and threats against regional energy infrastructure escalating. For building systems and data center operators, sustained energy price pressure is a real operational variable to watch heading into Q2.

🚀 Bonus — NASA's Artemis II crew is nearly halfway to the moon on the first crewed lunar mission since 1972. Four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft are in translunar transit after launching April 2nd — a genuinely historic moment.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 3, 2026
morning.content:
**"You were born with wings. Why prefer to crawl through life?"**
— Rumi

Good morning Zach. Here's what's happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — Major tariff announcements from the White House targeting imports from 185+ countries. Markets reacting sharply — Dow futures down significantly overnight. Broad economic implications across manufacturing and consumer goods sectors.

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — Data center construction demand continues surging across Texas. Multiple hyperscale facilities breaking ground in DFW and Houston corridors — strong forward pipeline for commercial HVAC contractors in the region.

— Wally

AI Brief - Tuesday, April 14

- OpenAI closed a $110B raise at an $840B valuation and locked in an Amazon partnership worth up to $50B, cementing its position as the most valuable private company on earth.
- Anthropic's revenue exploded from $14B to $19B in just weeks during Q1 2026, signaling the AI monetization era is fully underway.
- Anthropic's new Claude Mythos Preview - built to detect software security flaws - was shelved after being deemed "too powerful" for public release, with US regulators now warning Wall Street of AI-driven cyber threats.
- US utilities are gearing up for a $1.4T spending wave over 5 years to power AI data centers, making energy infrastructure the next major AI battleground.

- Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 8, 2026
ai.content:
🤖 AI Brief — April 8, 2026

• Anthropic locks down Claude Mythos Preview — won't do broad release. Model is powerful enough to autonomously find critical exploits across major OS and browsers. Anthropic launched Project Glasswing with Amazon, Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Nvidia to use it defensively before attackers weaponize the same capabilities. The era of AI as an offensive cyber tool is here; labs know it.

• SpaceX acquires xAI for $250B — the biggest consolidation in AI history. Musk now controls rockets, satellites, Starlink data, and a frontier model. Vertically integrated from inference to orbit.

• Intel, Tesla, and SpaceX partner on Terafab — a joint AI chip manufacturing venture aimed at slashing the cost of large training runs. If it works, it breaks Nvidia's stranglehold on compute.

• OpenAI launches Safety Fellowship at $100K per researcher — stipends for external researchers tackling AI risk mitigation. Smart PR move, but also real: they need credibility before IPO, and safety talent is the arms race no one talks about.

• Samsung Q1 profit +700% on AI memory — HBM chips for AI training/inference are printing money. SK Hynix and Micron are right behind. AI infrastructure spend hasn't peaked.

• Greece bans social media under 15, effective Jan 2027 — pushes EU for a bloc-wide age-verification framework. TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and Snapchat are all in the crosshairs. This policy pattern travels.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 7, 2026
ai.content:
🤖 AI Brief — April 7, 2026

• Claude Opus 4.6 takes the top spot. Anthropic's latest just hit #1 on LMSYS Chatbot Arena, beating GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro in human preference evals. SWE-bench score: 65.3% — a genuine step change in agentic coding. Architecture upgrade: hybrid transformer + sparse MoE for routing reasoning-heavy tasks.

• GPT-5.4 gets a meaningful update. OpenAI pushed a major refresh: 40% fewer refusals on benign edge cases, extended context handling, and better multi-doc analysis. GPT-5.4 Mini also upgraded — coding performance nearly on par with the full model at a fraction of the cost.

• Gemini 3.1 Pro goes GA on Vertex AI with 2M context. Enterprise production-ready now. Highlights: full-book/codebase-level caching, native video understanding at 1fps, and live web grounding via Google Search. Google is serious about enterprise.

• DeepSeek R2 drops and it's a shot across the bow. 92.7% on AIME 2025, 89.4% on MATH-500 — rivaling OpenAI o3. Priced ~70% cheaper than comparable Western models. Open-weight 32B distill also released. The China cost-efficiency gap is not closing — it's widening.

• Claude Mythos (Capybara) rollout begins. Anthropic confirmed the above-Opus model is entering deliberate phased release. Described internally as 'dramatically better at code and reasoning' with 'far ahead of any AI in cyber capabilities.' This is the one to watch.

• Llama 4 Scout ships for edge deployment. Meta open-sourced a 17B vision-language model that runs on a single 24GB GPU or M4 Pro — image, video, and PDF inputs included. Open-source is fully in the game now.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 6, 2026
ai.content:
🤖 AI Brief — April 6, 2026

• Claude Mythos confirmed. After a March 26 data leak exposed internal docs, Anthropic officially acknowledged "Capybara" — a new model tier above Opus. Internal testing says it's "dramatically better" at code and reasoning, and "currently far ahead of any AI in cyber capabilities." Being rolled out in deliberate phases. This is a genuine step change.

• OpenAI publishes its economic manifesto. Today the $852B company released a policy paper advocating robot taxes, public wealth funds, and a 4-day work week to offset AI-driven job displacement. Politically timed ahead of midterms — part wishlist, part positioning.

• OpenAI IPO in doubt. CFO Sarah Friar raised concerns today about Altman's plan to go public by late 2026, citing rising server costs, slowing revenue growth, and readiness questions. Meanwhile Brad Lightcap is out of the COO role and Fidji Simo is on medical leave — notable leadership churn at a pivotal moment.

• Gemini 3.1 Pro leads benchmarks. Google is topping 13 of 16 standard evals. Anthropic, OpenAI (GPT-5.4), and xAI (Grok 4.20 multi-agent) are all in striking distance — the frontier has never been this competitive or this close.

• Anthropic pulling back from third-party tools. Claude subscriptions no longer cover platforms like OpenClaw — users must pay via API or bundles. Reason given: compute demand straining capacity. Read between the lines: they're rationing for Mythos rollout.

• Q1 2026 VC: $300B into AI. Up 150%+ YoY. The entire venture market is AI. Crunchbase called it "unlike any quarter in history."

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 5, 2026
ai.content:
🤖 AI Brief — April 5, 2026

• SpaceX acquires xAI for $250B — the biggest consolidation move in AI history. Grok gets SpaceX compute and Starlink data. Musk now controls the rockets, satellites, and the AI model. That's a vertically integrated civilization.

• Anthropic drops Claude Mythos 5 — the world's first 10-trillion-parameter model. Built for cybersecurity, research, and long-range planning tasks where smaller models fall apart. This is a new capability tier.

• GPT-5.4 "Thinking" crosses the human threshold on OS-level tasks — 75% on OSWorld (up 27.7 points from GPT-5.2). AI can now reliably operate your desktop. Agentic workflows just became a production reality.

• OpenAI's $122B round is getting complicated — shares are "almost impossible" to move on secondary markets while Anthropic is oversubscribed. Smart money is quietly voting with its feet.

• Google's TurboQuant cuts inference memory by 6x — efficiency breakthroughs like this are what drop AI costs fast. Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is already 2.5x faster than its predecessor.

• Utah gave AI a medical license — first state to let AI autonomously renew drug prescriptions. Either a brilliant fix for healthcare access or a preview of things that will go wrong.

• Q1 2026 VC: $297B into AI startups — up ~150% quarter over quarter. The entire venture market is AI right now.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 4, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 4, 2026:

• Llama 4 is here and it's closing the gap — Meta's latest open-source release is now benchmark-competitive with the closed frontier models (GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro). Open-source is no longer playing catch-up.

• AI is building AI — OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepMind are all racing to ship self-improving research systems. Anthropic says Claude now writes ~90% of their internal code. The recursion is real.

• Investor sentiment is quietly shifting — despite OpenAI's record $122B raise, secondary market demand for OpenAI shares is collapsing while Anthropic is nearly impossible to get into. The smart money is moving.

• GPT-5.5 "Spud" confirmed for Q2 — OpenAI is maintaining a faster release cadence than anyone expected after already shipping 5.3 and 5.4 in the same week.

• Grok 4.20 introduces multi-agent architecture — not just a smarter model, but a new design pattern where Grok coordinates fleets of specialized sub-agents. Could be the next paradigm shift.

• OpenAI planning to nearly double headcount in 2026 (~1,500+ hires) — signals aggressive productization push as IPO pressure mounts.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 3, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 3, 2026:

• Google released Gemma 4 today — four open-weight models (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) under Apache 2.0 license. The 31B model is currently #3 open model in the world. Built on same research as Gemini 3. 400M+ developer downloads of the Gemma family to date.

• Apache 2.0 licensing is the headline — anyone can use commercially for free. Largest gap-closing between open and closed frontier models yet.

• Mobile-first focus: E2B and E4B sizes designed to run on-device. Next-gen Gemini Nano will be built on Gemma 4 architecture.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 2, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 2, 2026:

• OpenAI closes $122B round at $852B valuation — largest VC raise in history (surpasses the $110B figure; round grew).

• Q1 2026: $300B+ into AI startups, up 150%+ YoY — AI is the entire VC market right now.

• Anthropic leaked Claude Code source — signing system cracked within 24 hours of release.

• Microsoft building own frontier models by 2027 — reducing OpenAI dependency, major strategic shift.

• Meta unveils prescription AI smart glasses — consumer AI wearables accelerating.

• Rebellions (South Korea) raises $400M pre-IPO to challenge Nvidia on chips.

• Oracle, Amazon, Meta announcing major AI-driven layoffs — ~25K at Oracle alone.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 1, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 1, 2026:

• Oracle cut 30,000 jobs (18% of workforce) to fund AI data centers — directly relevant to Hunton Group HT data center business.

• OpenAI $110B round closed (Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia, SoftBank) — largest private capital raise in history.

• Q1 2026: $297B into ~6,000 startups globally — up 150% QoQ; AI buildout accelerating.

• Anthropic 'Claude Mythos' leaked — potential new capability tier beyond Opus, no official confirmation.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — March 30, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for March 30, 2026:

• OpenAI killed Sora — $15M/day operating costs against only $2.1M in lifetime revenue. Rare public admission of unit economics failure.

• March model avalanche: GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Ultra, Grok 4.20, and Mistral Small 4 all dropped within the same week. Leapfrogging continues at pace.

• MCP (Model Context Protocol) hit 97M installs — becoming the de facto standard for tool/context integration across AI systems.

• OpenAI raised $110B — largest private tech funding round in history.

— Wally
